metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap — agy --continue appends to the SAME conv-id (live-verified); scaffold driver stubs wait_turn/exit_clean/resume.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** `agy --help` documents two resume mechanisms: `--continue` / `-c` (Continue the most recent conversation) and `--conversation <ID>` (Resume a previous conversation by ID). **Live-verified**: after a clean exit, launching `agy --continue` in the same cwd appended the second turn to the SAME conv-id transcript (`f4826a94-…/transcript.jsonl` went 4→6 lines; NO new conversation dir was minted).

**daemon=full.** Because resume reuses the same `<conv-id>` directory, `sessionIDFromPath` (`adapter.go:58`) returns the SAME session_id across both PID lifetimes — the daemon sees the original session row re-appear (not a new row), exactly the scenario's load-bearing assertion. DiscoverPID rebinds the new agy PID to the same session by cwd (`pid.go:19`). This is the strongest of the lifecycle cells: the make-or-break emission fact (same transcript file on resume) is confirmed live, not just from code.

**driver=gap.** The scaffold driver stubs `wait_turn`, `exit_clean`, and `resume` as `not_implemented`. `record` must port them — the `resume` arm relaunches `agy --continue` (or `--conversation <id>`) in the SAME cwd so agy appends to the original transcript.
caveats: Resume relies on the cwd handoff: both lifetimes must use the same cwd so DiscoverPID rebinds the new PID to the same session. The resume launch must reuse the original RUN_CWD.
Antigravity emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; turn_done is inferred from a terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls.
observations omit cost/tokens — antigravity's JSONL carries no usage.
--continue resumes the MOST RECENT conversation in the cwd; if the driver runs concurrent conversations in one cwd, prefer --conversation <id> to target the right one.

sources.note: --continue / -c (Continue the most recent conversation); --conversation (Resume a previous conversation by ID)
sessionIDFromPath returns the same session_id across resume (same conv-id dir)
live: agy --continue appended turn 2 to the same conv-id transcript (4→6 lines), no new conv minted

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH (1.0.10), Google OAuth authenticated; no API key
irrlichd recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_HOME + alt port)
DRIVER GAP: wait_turn, exit_clean, resume are not_implemented in driver-interactive.sh — record ports them first
setup: Driver launches agy --dangerously-skip-permissions under tmux in a fresh cwd; agy mints its own conv-id (derived from the brain dir).
The resume step relaunches agy --continue (or --conversation <conv-id>) in the SAME cwd so agy appends to the original conv-id transcript.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: again

verify: events.jsonl shows the FIRST launch's session entering ready, then the working/ready cycle for turn 1
events.jsonl contains a process_exited for the session after turn 1 (clean exit)
the SAME session_id (same conv-id) re-appears after the resume launch — NOT a new session row
second-lifetime working transition fires on the SAME session_id, then back to ready
final session state is ready; tool calls == 0
